---
title: "iptables drops dangling connections"
date: 2007-05-25
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by jcpunk on 2007-05-25
This system is a squid proxy, here is my list of iptables rules

```

# iptables-save

# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.5
*mangle
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [845:323312]
:INPUT ACCEPT [5342165:3498358461]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [6409971:4240650934]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [6409601:4240461655]
:LOGACTTCP - [0:0]
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags ACK,URG URG -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,ACK FIN -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,RST FIN,RST -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN FIN,SYN -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,PSH,ACK,URG FIN,PSH,URG -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,PSH,ACK,URG FIN,SYN,RST,ACK,URG -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,PSH,ACK,URG FIN,SYN,RST,PSH,ACK,URG -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,PSH,ACK,URG NONE -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags PSH,ACK PSH -j LOGACTTCP 
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN,RST -j LOGACTTCP 
-A LOGACTTCP -j LOG --log-prefix "Bad TCP/IP DROP: " 
-A LOGACTTCP -j DROP 
COMMIT
#
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT DROP [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp ! --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -m state --state NEW -j LOG --log-prefix "Scan attempt? " 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp ! --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -m state --state NEW -j DROP 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp ! --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -m conntrack --ctstate INVALID -j LOG --log-prefix "Not New/Established " 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp ! --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -m conntrack --ctstate INVALID -j DROP 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,ACK SYN,ACK -m state --state NEW -j REJECT --reject-with tcp-reset 
-A INPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -m limit --limit 4/sec --limit-burst 9 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -j LOG --log-prefix "Ping abuse " 
-A INPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -j DROP 
-A INPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 113 -j REJECT --reject-with tcp-reset 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 3128 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -j LOG --log-prefix "Default DROP (INPUT): " 
-A INPUT -j DROP 
-A FORWARD -j LOG --log-prefix "Default DROP (FORWARD): " 
-A FORWARD -j DROP 
-A OUTPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -m limit --limit 9/sec --limit-burst 15 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -j LOG --log-prefix "Ping abuse from us " 
-A OUTPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -j DROP
-A OUTPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type any -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 53 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 53 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m multiport --dports 25,465,587 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m multiport --dports 80,443 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 123 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 123 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -j LOG --log-prefix "Default DROP (OUTPUT): " 
-A OUTPUT -j DROP 
COMMIT

```

syslog is full of lines similar to this:
```

Default DROP (OUTPUT): IN= OUT=eth0 SRC=192.168.12.4 DST=207.242.93.99 LEN=52 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=64 ID=34622 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=50808 DPT=80 WINDOW=3785 RES=0x00 ACK FIN URGP=0
```

It looks to me like squid wants to send a FIN ACK to the server, but iptables has already expired the session.  What an I do about this?

---

### Post by gustavoberman on 2007-05-25
so strange
you should check your conntrack modules to be loaded
also check /proc/net/ip_conntrack for that particular sessions to be assured

---

### Post by jcpunk on 2007-05-25
I also found this to be a bit odd...

Looks the the modules are loaded:

```
# lsmod |grep ip
iptable_mangle          3968  1 
ipt_REJECT              6784  5 
xt_multiport            4608  6 
ip_conntrack           53088  2 xt_conntrack,xt_state
nfnetlink               8088  1 ip_conntrack
ipt_LOG                 8320  16 
iptable_filter          4224  1 
ip_tables              15204  2 iptable_mangle,iptable_filter
x_tables               16132  9 ipt_ULOG,xt_conntrack,ipt_REJECT,xt_multiport,xt_limit,xt_state,xt_tcpudp,ipt_LOG,ip_tables

```

I will keep an eye on the bit in proc, I have been trying to use iptstate to follow what is going on, but the two together are more data...

Is there any other data on my end you would like/need to help me fumble my way through this?
